name: Kalaburagi
keySectors: Pulses & Dal Milling (~300 active mills, Tur Dal GI tag)
Agriculture (890,897 ha net sown area, 25.5% of GDDP)
Garments & Textiles (PM MITRA approved, 26,000 jobs target)
Pharma Packaging (proximity to Hyderabad Pharma City)
strengths: Largest tur dal producing district in India with GI tag (GI #353)
~300 active dal mills providing processing base
PM MITRA textile park: 1,000 acres, DPR finalized, Rs 50.9 Cr released
Massive limestone reserves (2.5-4 billion MT, 200-400 year supply)
Low land and labor costs compared to Bangalore/Hyderabad
challenges: Lowest per capita income in Karnataka (Rs 1,43,360 vs state avg Rs 3,10,000)
Very low irrigation coverage (13.4% vs state average)
High out-migration to Bangalore (43% of migrants)
Low CD ratio (64.5% vs state average 78.2%)
Limited industrial infrastructure and CETP facilities
Dal mill upgradation only 15% complete (45/300 active mills)
opportunities: PM MITRA textile park can create 26,000 direct jobs
Aerospace component manufacturing leveraging Hyderabad proximity
Pharma packaging for Hyderabad Pharma City Phase 2

kdem.brandPosition: Agritech & Biotech Hub for North Karnataka
digitalProximity: 500+ dal mills ready for agritech digitization
NH-44/NH-50 corridor connectivity to Bengaluru & Hyderabad tech hubs

policies.name: LEAP Agritech Incubation
policies.relevance: Rs 1,000 Cr fund, active in Kalaburagi

kpis.title: Credit Disbursement
details: Priority sector: Rs 2,750 cr (79% of target)

kpis.title: Dal Mill Upgradation
details: Value addition: Rs 180 cr (Target: Rs 2,400 cr)

reasons: Low and uncertain income from farming
Limited non-farm jobs in the district

components.name: Rural Connectivity
items: All-weather roads to dal mills: 80/100 km

components.name: Agro-Processing Clusters
items: Value addition: Rs 180 cr (Target: Rs 1,600 cr)

phase3.name: Scale
phase3.description: Strategic industrial attraction, healthcare/education upgrades, and urban infrastructure development.

financing.details: RIDF, PMKSY, PMFME, Smart Cities, AMRUT

sectors.name: Garments & Textiles

risks.mitigation: ITI training, attract women workers, return migrants
Direct engagement with Ministry of Textiles

sectors.recommendation: PROCEED IMMEDIATELY - Funding approved, fastest job creation

sectors.name: Limestone & Cement
sectors.recommendation: PROCEED - Resource proven, but regulatory timeline critical

timeline.phase1: 2026-2028: Land, ITI curriculum, first 5 factories, AS9100 consultant

risks.mitigation: Triple-layer quality, German/Israeli partnership, 100% CMM inspection

risks.risk: AS9100 certification failure (60% first-time fail rate)
risks.mitigation: Consultant from Day 1, build compliance into factory design, mock audits

risks.risk: Long sales cycles (12-18 months OEM approval)
risks.mitigation: Start OEM outreach Year 1, parallel approvals

sectors.name: Pharma Packaging

timeline.phase1: 2026-2027: Land, Schedule M consultant, pharma MoUs
timeline.phase2: 2027-2028: First 5 factories, Schedule M licensing, trial production

risks.risk: Schedule M license delays (6-9 months normal, 12-18 if issues)
risks.mitigation: Hire ex-CDSCO inspector, mock audits quarterly, build right from Day 1
Triple-check quality, 100% vision inspection, zero-defect guarantee
Diversify to Bangalore 15%, export 30%, other cities 10%

sectors.recommendation: PROCEED - Perfect timing for Pharma City Phase 2 (2029-2030)

infrastructureStatus.roads: Good - Internal roads paved, NH connectivity 8km

power.gap: 50 MVA substation needed for PM MITRA

roads.gap: Sedam IA approach road needs widening

effluent.gap: CETP mandatory for PM MITRA, pharma clusters

challenges.impact: Need Rs 392 cr additional credit to match state average
Insufficient lending for Phase 1 industrial expansion
Rural areas underserved, especially for FPO financing

bottlenecks.impact: Critical - delays PM MITRA, aerospace projects

dataSources.name: Agmarknet (DMIC, Ministry of Agriculture)
fields: Commodity Prices (Tur Dal, Bengal Gram, Green Gram)
